parameters-en.Time: Adjusts the length of time for the 4-tap delay sequence to repeat. When set to note values, Time follows the system tempo.
parameters-en.Feedback: Controls how many times the 4-tap delay sequence repeats. When set to 0%, the sequence is heard only once. At high values, the delay taps may begin to self-oscillate.
parameters-en.Diffusion: Controls the amount of smearing between discrete echoes, sometimes resulting in a softer, less percussive repeats.
parameters-en.Low Cut: Applies a low cut (or high pass) filter to the repeats, letting you remove the effected signal below a certain frequency.
parameters-en.High Cut: Applies a high cut (or low pass) filter to the repeats, letting you remove the effected signal above a certain frequency.
parameters-en.Mix: Controls the blend between the delay repeats and the dry signal. At 0%, no repeats are heard; at 100%, no dry signal is heard.
parameters-en.Level: Sets the overall level of the block.
parameters-en.T1 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 500 ms, and T1 Scale is set to 25%, tap 1's delay is 125ms (or 25% of 500 ms).
parameters-en.T1 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 1 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T1 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 1. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T2 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 500 ms, and T2 Scale is set to 50%, tap 2's delay is 250ms (or 50% of 500 ms).
parameters-en.T2 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 2 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T2 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 2. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T3 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 500 ms, and T3 Scale is set to 75%, tap 3's delay is 375ms (or 75% of 500 ms).
parameters-en.T3 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 3 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T3 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 3. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T4 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 500 ms, and T4 Scale is set to 100%, tap 4's delay is also 500ms (or 100% of 500 ms).
parameters-en.T4 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 4 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T4 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 4. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.Mod Mode: Selects the type of modulation applied to the repeats--None (Off), Chorus, or Vibrato.
parameters-en.Rate: Controls the speed or rate of the modulation applied to the repeats.
parameters-en.Depth: Controls the depth or intensity of the modulation applied to the repeats.
parameters-en.Spread: Subtly controls the apparent stereo width of the modulation.
